notes: Architect Agent initialized. Beginning design phase for mini-google-v2 web crawler and search engine system.
All requirements parsed. Key constraints: language-native HTTP, no Scrapy/Elasticsearch/Whoosh, SQLite only, single machine.

rationale: asyncio event loop for orchestration; ThreadPoolExecutor for blocking urllib HTTP calls via run_in_executor; asyncio.Queue as BFS frontier with natural back-pressure.
stdlib-only; satisfies language-native constraint; runs cleanly inside ThreadPoolExecutor threads.
put_nowait prevents deadlock when all workers try to enqueue simultaneously; drop-on-full with skipped counter is transparent to user.
Token bucket allows bursts up to capacity while enforcing average rate; pure asyncio keeps event loop unblocked.
WAL enables concurrent reads during writes (search + crawl simultaneously); thread-local connections eliminate all thread-safety concerns without locks.

choice: TF-IDF with exact match weight=3 and prefix match weight=1
rationale: TF-IDF is simpler to implement correctly in pure Python+SQLite; external search libs explicitly prohibited; exact/prefix weighting differentiates strong vs weak matches.
FastAPI is async-native (integrates with asyncio crawler), provides automatic validation, StreamingResponse for SSE, and minimal overhead.
SSE is unidirectional server?client which matches the stats push use case; simpler than WebSockets; native EventSource support in browsers.

choice: Vanilla JS single-page HTML file, no CDN dependencies
rationale: Zero build step; works fully offline; 3-tab SPA is trivial in vanilla JS; serves from FastAPI StaticFiles; no CDN requests.

choice: Two-layer: permanent visited dedup + session queue snapshot
alternatives_rejected: restart from depth 0 with visited dedup only
rationale: Layer 1 (visited table) prevents re-indexing; Layer 2 (queue snapshot JSON in crawl_sessions) skips re-discovering already-found URLs. Both layers needed for efficient deep-crawl resume.

notes: word_url_tf covering index is the most performance-critical — enables search queries to read tf without extra join.
All public methods have defined signatures and documented return types. No circular dependencies in module graph.
Full request/response shapes defined in PRD Section 11. Error codes specified for all endpoints.
PRD is 600+ lines covering all requirements. All interface contracts specified. Agents 02-06 can begin implementation in parallel.

key_decisions.thread_safety: threading.local() per-thread SQLite connection; WAL mode for concurrent read+write
key_decisions.schema: word_index stores frequency INTEGER (not TF REAL); dedicated crawl_queue_state table instead of JSON blob
key_decisions.scoring: (frequency*10) + (1000 if exact else 0) - (depth*5) per token per match; aggregated in Python across tokens
key_decisions.search_strategy: single SQL query per token using OR + CASE WHEN for exact/prefix tagging; Python aggregation
key_decisions.migrations: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN in _run_migrations(); OperationalError silently ignored for idempotency
key_decisions.changes_check: SELECT changes() called before commit() to reliably detect INSERT OR IGNORE outcome
key_decisions.column_whitelist: update_session_stats() validates column names against explicit set before SQL interpolation

notes: Full production implementation. No TODO stubs. All edge cases handled. Schema reconciled between PRD and task spec ť see agents/03_storage_agent.md Section 3.3 for differences.

key_decisions.concurrency: asyncio workers + ThreadPoolExecutor for HTTP; mark_visited serialised on event loop thread for lock-free dedup
key_decisions.stop_mechanism: asyncio.wait races queue.join() vs poll_stop() coroutine; stop_event detected within 50ms; workers cancelled cleanly
key_decisions.task_done_invariant: CancelledError path calls task_done() explicitly before return; finally block guards against ValueError on double-call
key_decisions.rate_limiter: threading.Lock token bucket; try_acquire() spinloop with await asyncio.sleep(0.05) keeps event loop free
key_decisions.url_filtering: _should_skip_url() checks extensions/prefixes/query-patterns before consuming rate-limit token
key_decisions.snapshot_drain: _finalize() drains asyncio.Queue from event loop thread using get_nowait(); calls SessionDB.save_queue_snapshot()
key_decisions.parser_design: TextParser tracks opaque-tag depth counter; skips script/style/noscript/head; word_counts() returns Counter of tokenize() output
key_decisions.resume: find_incomplete_session() checked on start; snapshot restored; falls back to origin seed if snapshot empty

notes: Full production implementation. No TODO stubs. Storage interface consumed from storage.database and storage.index per architect contracts.

key_decisions.event_loop_isolation: AsyncCrawler background thread has its own asyncio loop; FastAPI runs on main loop; all cross-boundary calls use threading.Event and threading.Lock only ť no asyncio primitives shared
key_decisions.app_state: Module-level AppState singleton populated at FastAPI startup event; holds InvertedIndex, SessionDB, FailedURLDB, QueueStateDB, and optional AsyncCrawler reference
key_decisions.pause_endpoint: POST /api/pause is a toggle (pause if running, resume if paused); POST /api/resume added as explicit non-toggle complement
key_decisions.session_id_race: await asyncio.sleep(0.15) + session_db.get_active_session() used to retrieve session_id after non-blocking crawler.start() returns
key_decisions.sse_design: Async generator yields data every 2s; asyncio.CancelledError caught silently on client disconnect; json.dumps(default=str) handles float timestamps
key_decisions.static_mount: StaticFiles mounted at /static after all API route definitions; GET / serves static/index.html with fallback HTML if missing
key_decisions.reset_scope: DELETE /api/reset calls index.reset() which wipes pages/word_index/visited; crawl_sessions and failed_urls preserved for history
key_decisions.schema_reconciliation: Actual DB uses origin not origin_url, depth not max_depth, REAL timestamps not TEXT ť API returns dict(row) values verbatim so consumers see actual column names

notes: Full production implementation. No TODO stubs. Two-event-loop architecture strictly enforced. All endpoints match PRD Section 11 plus task-spec extensions. Swagger docs at /docs.
UI finalized with indexing controls, search panel, live status cards, and SSE-based updates.

issues_found: CLI stats key mismatch with crawler snapshot

fixes: Normalized CLI stat keys with backward-compatible fallback mapping

notes: All requested assignment artifacts are present in mini-google-v2.
